title: Smoky Spicy Venezuelan Bowtie Pasta
description: This brunch-mood dish combines fluffy bowtie pasta with a blend of smoky and spicy flavors, perfect for a light meal that's both nourishing and flavorful.

ingredients.name: Bowtie Pasta
ingredients.notes: Ensure it is cooked according to package instructions

ingredients.name: Tofu Crumbles
ingredients.notes: Use firm tofu, drain and crumble into small pieces

ingredients.name: Red Onion
ingredients.notes: Chopped finely to release more flavors

ingredients.name: Green Chilis (jalapenos or serranos)
ingredients.notes: Finely chopped and seeded for extra spiciness

instructions: Cook the bowtie pasta in salted boiling water until al dente, then drain and set aside.
In a large skillet or pan, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add chopped green chilis and sauté for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add minced garlic and cook for another minute to soften slightly.
Fold in crumbled tofu, paprika, cumin, salt, and pepper. Stir well to combine all ingredients thoroughly.
Return the cooked bowtie pasta to the skillet. Toss gently to coat with the spice mixture evenly.
Transfer the pasta to a baking dish. Bake at 400°F for 15 minutes or until bubbly and golden brown on top.
Sprinkle fresh basil leaves over the top if using, before serving.
